mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing units starts signals a strengthening demand for residential construction, which is positively correlated with S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The 20-day forward return correlation with HOUST falls below 0.2 over any 6-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in industrial production signals a weakening supply chain, which is negatively correlated with S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below 100 for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: The 60-day forward return correlation with INDPRO remains above 0.15 over any 9-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in payroll employment signals a strengthening labor market, which is positively correlated with S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The 5-day forward return correlation with PAYEMS falls below 0.1 over any 3-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in the federal funds rate signals a tightening monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The 20-day forward return correlation with FEDFUNDS remains above 0.2 over any 6-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in real GDP signals a strengthening demand for goods and services, which is positively correlated with S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The 60-day forward return correlation with GDPC1 falls below 0.15 over any 9-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in the unemployment rate signals a weakening labor market, which is negatively correlated with S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The 5-day forward return correlation with UNRATE remains above 0.1 over any 3-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in real GDP and a 1-month increase in housing units starts signals a strengthening demand for goods and services and residential construction, which is positively correlated with S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The 20-day forward return correlation with the joint signal of GDPC1 and HOUST falls below 0.2 over any 6-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in the federal funds rate and a 1-month decline in the consumer price index signals a tightening monetary cycle and a weakening inflationary pressure, which is negatively correlated with S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises above 5.5% and CPIAUCSL falls below 310 for 1 month
failure_condition: The 60-day forward return correlation with the joint signal of FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL remains above 0.15 over any 9-month rolling window.
